messages.content:
YOUR ROLE: You are the partner running a pre-mortem on this market model for a
paying client. The client's own team has already read every number in the
ledger, including every sensitivity band. Your reputation rests on surfacing
what THEY would not list: risks that look benign on the surface but could kill
the venture, argued mechanistically, anchored in the model.

HARD REQUIREMENTS — a hypothesis that misses any of these will be rejected by
a validator before any human reads it:
1. targetNodes: real ledger node ids; perturbation ops only on FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes.
2. mechanism: at least 3 steps (trigger → propagation → model impact), EVERY
   step carrying refs — "ledger:<nodeId>", "digest:<group>#c<n>", or
   "profile:<field>". A step you cannot reference is a step you are guessing.
3. falsifier: the single concrete observation that would kill this risk. If you
   cannot name one, it is not a claim.
4. whyMissable: why a competent founder's own risk list omits this. "It's
   non-obvious" is not an argument; name the surface appearance that hides it.
5. likelihood: a probability with a basis ("evidence" | "base-rate" |
   "judgment") and a rationale. No naked numbers.
6. indicators: at least 2 observable early warnings, each with WHERE to watch
   (a named tender portal, an operator's capex call, a standards docket, a
   vendor's price list) and the reading that should trigger re-assessment.
7. mitigations: at least 2. Where the right move is to buy information, use
   type "information" and name the ledger node it de-risks (voiNodeId) — its
   value will be computed, not asserted.

QUOTAS (per lens): at least one hypothesis self-tagged expectedObviousness
"low", and at least one whose perturbation targets NONE of tamBase /
serviceableFactor / obtainableFactor — the tornado already covers those bands;
look past them.

MINE THE RESIDUALS: the highest-yield material is where sources disagree with
the curated value (the digests' "tensions"), where searches came back empty
(the digests' "absences" — an unquantified market is a risk, not a comfort),
where a derivation's crossCheck diverges from its primary method, and where a
precise-looking value carries maturity "needs-source".

HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

BANNED GENRES (emitting one of these wastes your slot):
- "Find 2 customers to talk to and get an LOI" — an action item, not a risk. It names no node, no mechanism, no falsifier.
- "Do research on customer buying behaviour" — a homework assignment any consultant could write for any company.
- "Competition is strong / the market is concentrated" — the ledger already states CR3. Restating a fact is not a risk; the risk is a MECHANISM that concentration enables.
- "The market may be smaller than expected" — the tamBase band (240–520) already says this. Only worth saying if you name WHICH derivation hop fails and WHY.
- "Regulatory changes could impact the business" — name the instrument, the compliance artifact it requires, and the date it lands, or drop it.
- "The band's low end might be true" with no new reason — the band is already in the model; you are paid for what is NOT in the model.

GOLD EXEMPLAR (the target register — do NOT repeat its content):
{
 "id": "busway-hollowing-hyperscale",
 "title": "Busway + OCP power shelves hollow out the hyperscale 44% from inside the scope boundary",
 "narrative": "The model excludes busbars and power shelves as out-of-scope substitutes — a scoping footnote. But hyperscale is 44% of TAM, and it is precisely the segment whose new AI-density builds are moving power distribution from rack PDUs to overhead busway feeding OCP-style in-rack power shelves. If that architecture becomes the default for new CE hyperscale capacity, the largest slice of this TAM is not 'ours to win slowly' — it is structurally shrinking while the headline number stays flat. The exclusion list quietly became the market thesis.",
 "category": "boundary",
 "targetNodes": [
  "seg.hyperscale"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "New hyperscale AI-density builds adopt busway + in-rack DC power shelves instead of discrete rack PDUs",
   "refs": [
    "digest:seg-mix#c?",
    "ledger:seg.hyperscale"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "CE hyperscale capacity additions (the growth the 9% CAGR is made of) carry falling rack-PDU content per MW; replacement demand in legacy halls does not offset it",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:shape.cagr",
    "digest:tam-base#c?"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The hyperscale share of the rack-PDU market shrinks in absolute terms — seg.hyperscale 0.44 overstates the addressable slice for a new entrant's window",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:seg.hyperscale"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "Substitutes were explicitly considered and excluded — the boundary decision looks handled. The kill path is not 'we misjudged the boundary' but 'the boundary moved into the biggest segment while the TAM base year stayed put'.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "2025–26 CE hyperscale fit-out specs (or Schneider/Vertiv rack-power order mixes) showing intelligent rack PDUs holding share in new AI-density halls.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.35,
  "rationale": "OCP power-shelf adoption is documented for AI racks, but CE colocation and enterprise retrofits (56% of TAM) keep buying rack PDUs; timing within a 12-month window is uncertain.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "seg.hyperscale",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.22,
   "note": "≈half of the hyperscale slice migrates to busway/shelf architectures"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Rack-power architecture specified in new CE hyperscale tenders (busway/shelf vs PDU)",
   "where": "Frankfurt/Amsterdam data-center fit-out tenders; OCP regional summit deployment talks",
   "threshold": "≥2 flagship CE builds specifying shelf-based distribution",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Incumbent rack-PDU revenue mix commentary (Vertiv/Schneider earnings)",
   "where": "Quarterly earnings calls, rack-power product-line commentary",
   "threshold": "PDU line flat while busway line grows double-digit",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Commission a CE rack-power architecture survey across the 2026 hyperscale build pipeline",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "seg.hyperscale"
  },
  {
   "action": "Qualify the product line for colocation/enterprise retrofit first, where PDU form-factor is sticky",
   "type": "strategic"
  }
 ]
}

content: {"hypotheses":[{"id":"framework-agreements-close-first-buyer","title":"The declared 'large operators' beachhead is pre-signed: global framework agreements close the 40% buyer cell before the first sales call","narrative":"The venture's own profile names large operators as the first buyer, and the ledger prices that cell at 0.40 of PDU spend with a comfortable 0.33–0.47 band. But the band models uncertainty about SIZE, not AVAILABILITY. The procurement record shows large operators buying rack power through multi-year global framework agreements and all-in integrated contracts (the Digital Realty–Schneider $373M framework pattern; Iren's all-in Dell deal; Jülich bundling PDU into a single IT line item). A framework agreement is not a competitor to out-sell — it is a contract that removes the tender from the market for its whole term. If a majority of the 0.40 cell is framework-governed, the entrant's declared beachhead is contractually closed for exactly its Year-1 window, and the YAM is silently re-based onto the slower mid-market and channel cells.","category":"competitive","targetNodes":["cust.operator-large","obtainableFactor"],"mechanism":[{"step":"trigger","text":"CE large operators renew or extend multi-year global rack-power framework agreements with Schneider/Vertiv/Eaton — the same vendors whose named major customers (Equinix, Telefonica, Vodafone) are the CE operator base","refs":["digest:cust-mix#c12","digest:cr3-vendors#c1","ledger:cust.operator-large"]},{"step":"propagation","text":"PDU line items ride the framework or the integrated OEM package; no standalone, contestable RFQ ever reaches the market for that operator's builds during the term — the entrant's pre-launch, first-12-months window (profile: Stage) coincides with mid-term, not renewal, for most frameworks","refs":["profile:Stage","ledger:cust.operator-large","digest:cust-mix#c0"]},{"step":"model-impact","text":"The addressable fraction of cust.operator-large collapses from 0.40 to roughly the framework-gap residual (~0.20); because the ledger's four channel values are unsourced internal estimates, nothing in the model currently resists this collapse","refs":["ledger:cust.operator-large","ledger:obtainableFactor","digest:cust-mix#c0"]}],"whyMissable":"'Large operators buy direct' reads as good news on the founder's risk list — direct means no channel margin and a nameable account list. The surface appearance of reachability (you know exactly who to call) hides the contract structure: direct purchasing at that scale is framework purchasing, and the sensitivity band on the node models estimation error, not contractual foreclosure.","expectedObviousness":"medium","falsifier":"Two or more CE large operators (Equinix/NTT/Data4-class) issuing standalone, multi-vendor rack-PDU RFQs open to non-framework vendors within the next 12 months.","likelihood":{"value":0.5,"rationale":"Multiple documented procurement artifacts (Digital Realty–Schneider framework, Iren all-in contract, Jülich bundled line item) all point the same direction; what is uncertain is the fraction of CE operator spend covered, not the mechanism's existence.","basis":"evidence"},"perturbation":[{"nodeId":"cust.operator-large","op":"set","value":0.2,"note":"Framework-governed spend removed from the addressable large-operator cell; residual is framework gaps, expiries and carve-outs"},{"nodeId":"obtainableFactor","op":"scale","value":0.7,"note":"Year-1 pace slows further because the remaining open demand sits in channels the venture did not plan to enter first"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"Global or EMEA rack-power framework renewals announced by Schneider/Vertiv/Eaton with named CE operators","where":"Schneider and Vertiv press rooms; DataCenterDynamics procurement coverage; operator annual reports (supplier commitments notes)","threshold":"Any CE major renewing a ≥3-year rack-power framework during the venture's launch year","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Standalone rack-PDU lots appearing in European public/colo tenders","where":"TED (tenders.europa.eu) CPV codes for power distribution equipment; national procurement portals (DE: bund.de vergabe)","threshold":"Fewer than 2 standalone, multi-vendor PDU lots per quarter across CE-7","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Structured interviews with 3 CE operator procurement leads specifically on framework coverage, term dates, and second-source carve-out clauses","type":"information","voiNodeId":"cust.operator-large","note":"Replaces the unsourced 0.40 with a framework-adjusted addressable share and yields an expiry calendar"},{"action":"Build the Year-1 account plan around framework expiry dates and approved-second-source slots rather than open-market wins","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Frameworks typically permit qualified alternates; entering as second source converts foreclosure into a wedge"},{"action":"Negotiate pilot clauses with operators' innovation/energy teams that sit outside procurement frameworks (retrofit metering pilots)","type":"contractual","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Pilot spend often bypasses framework governance thresholds"}]},{"id":"distributor-line-review-lockout","title":"Annual line reviews plus Legrand's dual-brand grip close the distributor cell mid-cycle — the entrant arrives with the shelf already allocated","narrative":"The 0.13 distributor cell looks like the easy channel: no framework agreements, documented master-distributor models, even explicit private-label options for distributors in buyer's guides. But distribution in this category runs on annual line reviews with exclusivity and margin-program terms, and the intelligent-PDU shelf specifically is consolidated — Legrand owns BOTH Raritan and Server Technology, the two intelligent-PDU specialist brands, giving one vendor two slots on every line card and strong leverage in review negotiations. A pre-launch entrant hits the market mid-cycle: even a willing distributor cannot onboard a conflicting line until the next review, which lands at or after the end of the venture's Year-1 window. The cell is not small — for the YAM horizon it is closed.","category":"competitive","targetNodes":["cust.distributor"],"mechanism":[{"step":"trigger","text":"Master-distributor structures (the MaxPower pattern: one distributor supplying all reseller partners) concentrate line-card decisions into few annual review events, and Legrand's ownership of both Raritan and Server Technology means the intelligent-PDU slots on those line cards are already double-occupied by one incumbent","refs":["digest:cust-mix#c7","digest:cr3-vendors#c4","ledger:cust.distributor"]},{"step":"propagation","text":"The venture launches mid-review-cycle (profile: pre-launch, first 12 months); the documented private-label route exists but requires certifications, stock commitments and margin programs a Year-1 entrant cannot yet fund, so no CE distributor can list the product inside the YAM window","refs":["profile:Stage","digest:cust-mix#c13","ledger:cust.distributor"]},{"step":"model-impact","text":"cust.distributor (0.13, maturity needs-source, zero corroborating evidence in the ledger) contributes nothing to Year-1 reachable demand; the funnel silently overstates the channel mix available inside the obtainable window","refs":["ledger:cust.distributor","ledger:obtainableFactor"]}],"whyMissable":"Distribution looks like the LOW-commitment channel — 'anyone can get listed eventually' — and the ledger's 0.13 looks too small to interrogate. The hidden variable is timing: line reviews are annual gates, so a cell that is genuinely open in steady state is closed for precisely the 12-month window the YAM measures. Founders model channel share, not channel calendar.","expectedObviousness":"low","falsifier":"A named CE electrical/IT distributor (Rexel, Sonepar, Also-class) confirming in writing that a new intelligent-PDU line can be onboarded within two quarters without waiting for the annual line review or triggering an exclusivity conflict.","likelihood":{"value":0.45,"rationale":"Annual line reviews with incumbency advantages are the documented norm in electrical distribution (base-rate), and the Legrand dual-brand consolidation of the intelligent-PDU shelf is a hard fact; the open question is how rigidly CE distributors enforce review timing for a differentiated product.","basis":"base-rate"},"perturbation":[{"nodeId":"cust.distributor","op":"exclude","value":null,"note":"Distributor cell unavailable to the venture within the Year-1 horizon — foreclosed by review-cycle timing and shelf exclusivity, not shrunk"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"Intelligent-PDU brands listed on CE distributor line cards and webshops","where":"Rexel Germany / Sonepar online catalogs; Also and Ingram Micro DC-infrastructure category pages","threshold":"Raritan + Server Technology + APC occupying all intelligent-PDU listings with no non-incumbent brand added over two quarters","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Legrand channel-program announcements tightening intelligent-PDU distribution terms","where":"Legrand investor communications and EMEA partner-program pages; distribution trade press (ChannelPartner.de)","threshold":"Any announced exclusivity, rebate-tier or dual-brand bundling program covering Raritan/Server Technology in EMEA","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Commission a channel audit: map line-review dates, exclusivity terms and private-label requirements across the top 5 CE electrical/IT distributors","type":"information","voiNodeId":"cust.distributor","note":"Turns an assumed 0.13 cell into a dated calendar of when (if ever) it opens"},{"action":"Enter via a distributor's private-label program (documented option) rather than as a branded line — sidesteps the brand-slot conflict","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Buyer's-guide evidence shows distributors actively seek OEM/private-label intelligent-PDU options"},{"action":"Time distributor outreach to the review calendar and pre-book slots for Year-2 rather than burning Year-1 sales capacity on a closed gate","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":null}]},{"id":"bundle-pricing-kills-bakeoffs","title":"Near-zero-margin PDU bundling inside power+cooling packages means the contestable Year-1 pool is below the model's own 0.5% floor","narrative":"The obtainableFactor band (0.5–3%) assumes there exists a pool of standalone rack-PDU purchase decisions a new entrant can compete for. But the same incumbents sell the $23B power and $26B cooling packages those PDUs plug into, and the procurement record shows PDUs travelling as embedded line items inside those packages — where the incumbent can price the PDU at near-zero margin because the busway, UPS and cooling carry the deal. The only standalone PDU procurements surfaced by research were tiny ($58K–$130K), had 1–2 bidders, and one was explicitly sole-sourced. Meanwhile the 1%-of-SAM anchor itself is generic startup folklore: the sources define SOM as a 1–3 or 3–5 year cumulative window, and the closest worked hardware comparable runs at ~0.1% of SAM. Both legs of the 1% — the benchmark and the tender evidence — point below the band, not inside it.","category":"execution","targetNodes":["obtainableFactor"],"mechanism":[{"step":"trigger","text":"Incumbents holding both the power ($23B) and cooling ($26B) packages bundle intelligent PDUs into integrated deals, cross-subsidising PDU price to zero incremental margin — no bake-off is ever held on the PDU line","refs":["digest:cust-mix#c2","digest:cust-mix#c3","ledger:shape.cr3"]},{"step":"propagation","text":"The residual standalone-tender pool is small and often non-contestable (sole-sourced, 1–2 bidders), while the 1% Year-1 benchmark the node anchors on is defined by its own sources as a multi-year cumulative figure — Prospeo frames SOM as 1–3 years, Wise as 3–5, and TryBuildCo's worked example lands at ~0.1% of SAM","refs":["digest:obtainable-benchmark#c1","digest:obtainable-benchmark#c6","digest:obtainable-benchmark#c7"]},{"step":"model-impact","text":"True Year-1 obtainable share of SAM falls to ~0.3% — below the band's 0.5% floor — because the denominator (SAM) includes bundled demand that never becomes a contestable decision, and the Tractian cross-check that supported ≤1% is itself unverified against its source ($200K starting revenue, not $1.2M first-year)","refs":["ledger:obtainableFactor","digest:obtainable-benchmark#c10","digest:obtainable-benchmark#c0"]}],"whyMissable":"The founder's team already 'corrected' this node once (3%→1%) and the band looks conservative, so it reads as the model's most stress-tested number. The hidden failure is compositional: the band debates WHICH percentage of SAM is winnable, while the bundling mechanism removes demand from the contestable denominator entirely — a smaller pie of decisions, not a smaller slice of spend.","expectedObviousness":"medium","falsifier":"A logged pipeline of ≥5 standalone, multi-vendor rack-PDU tenders in CE-7, each ≥€250K, posted within any 6-month window, in which a non-incumbent vendor is qualified to bid.","likelihood":{"value":0.4,"rationale":"The bundling evidence (integrated contracts, sole-sourced standalone bids) and the benchmark-window semantics both push the same direction, but retrofit and colo tenant purchases do generate some genuinely standalone PDU decisions that the tender search may have under-sampled.","basis":"evidence"},"perturbation":[{"nodeId":"obtainableFactor","op":"set","value":0.003,"note":"Contestable-decision pool shrinks the effective Year-1 capture below the modeled 0.5% floor"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"Ratio of bundled vs standalone rack-PDU procurement in published CE tenders","where":"TED (tenders.europa.eu) — compare PDU-specific CPV lots vs PDU lines inside integrated electrical-fit-out lots","threshold":"≥80% of PDU volume appearing only inside integrated lots over two quarters","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Incumbent commentary on solutions/portfolio attach rates for rack power","where":"Vertiv and Schneider quarterly earnings calls; Schneider capital markets day materials","threshold":"Explicit statements that rack PDU is sold predominantly as part of integrated rack/power/cooling solutions","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Build a bottom-up contestable-tender census for CE-7 (12 months of TED + colo tenant RFQs) to replace the generic 1% benchmark with a counted pipeline","type":"information","voiNodeId":"obtainableFactor","note":"Directly converts the node from folklore benchmark to observed decision volume"},{"action":"Target the purchase moments bundles don't reach: colo TENANT-side PDU purchases and brownfield retrofit/metering upgrades, where no incumbent package exists to hide the PDU inside","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"Retrofit demand is decision-rich even when new-build demand is bundled"},{"action":"Price and package for attach to third-party rack integrators' bundles rather than head-to-head against incumbent bundles","type":"operational","voiNodeId":null,"note":null}]},{"id":"oem-cell-two-sided-squeeze","title":"The OEM/integrator cell is squeezed shut from both sides: brand-spec deals go to incumbent ecosystems, white-label seats go to scale ODMs","narrative":"The 0.22 OEM/integrator cell looks like the natural entry channel for an industrial entrant — and superficially the evidence agrees, because BOTH routes into it are documented: integrators buy branded PDUs, and buyer's guides advertise OEM/private-label options. The trap is that each route is closed for a different reason. Every integrator case study found shows selection of branded incumbents on certification, DCIM compatibility and support ecosystems — assets a Year-1 entrant does not have. The white-label seats, meanwhile, are held by Asian ODMs (the Digipower pattern) competing on a manufacturing cost curve a Central European industrial entrant cannot match. The cell's 0.22 value is an unsourced internal estimate with an addressability premise no source supports; for this venture the genuinely open fraction may be a third of the modeled cell.","category":"competitive","targetNodes":["cust.oem"],"mechanism":[{"step":"trigger","text":"Rack integrators select PDU brands on features, certification and DCIM-ecosystem compatibility — the documented integrator behavior favors incumbents whose monitoring stacks are already deployed — while the private-label demand that does exist is served by dedicated OEM/ODM manufacturers with global scale","refs":["digest:cust-mix#c14","digest:cust-mix#c13","ledger:cust.oem"]},{"step":"propagation","text":"An industrial entrant sits in the dead zone between the two: no installed DCIM/support ecosystem to win brand-spec designs (those accrue to the Legrand/Raritan/Server Technology axis and the top-5 vendor set) and no ODM cost position to win price-spec white-label seats","refs":["digest:cr3-vendors#c4","digest:cr3-vendors#c1","profile:Company"]},{"step":"model-impact","text":"Addressable share of cust.oem falls from 0.22 to ~0.08 (the fraction of integrator demand that is regional, spec-flexible, and not price-anchored to ODM quotes); the funnel's channel mix shifts further onto cells that are themselves contested","refs":["ledger:cust.oem","ledger:cust.operator-large"]}],"whyMissable":"The cell appears doubly open — 'we can sell branded OR white-label' — which is exactly why it survives founder scrutiny. Two documented routes in feels like redundancy; it is actually two separately-owned gates, and the evidence that each gate is held (integrator brand loyalty; ODM incumbency) sits in different source types that never appear on the same page of a risk review.","expectedObviousness":"low","falsifier":"One CE rack integrator or OEM signing a design-in or white-label agreement with the entrant, or a documented integrator RFQ in which a non-incumbent, non-ODM intelligent PDU is evaluated on merit within the launch year.","likelihood":{"value":0.4,"rationale":"Every retrieved integrator case study shows incumbent-brand selection and no source quantifies an open OEM share, but the sample of case studies is small and integrator behavior for differentiated intelligent-PDU features is untested — judgment on how far the pattern generalizes.","basis":"judgment"},"perturbation":[{"nodeId":"cust.oem","op":"set","value":0.08,"note":"Only the regional, spec-flexible integrator fraction remains addressable; brand-spec and ODM-price fractions foreclosed"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"PDU brands named in European integrator/turnkey rack case studies and reference designs","where":"Vertiv, Legrand, Rittal and Schleifenbauer project case-study pages; DataCenterDynamics build coverage","threshold":"≥3 consecutive quarters of new CE integrator projects specifying only incumbent-brand PDUs","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"ODM intelligent-PDU pricing and OEM-program activity","where":"Digipower and comparable ODM price lists / OEM program pages; Computex and Data Centre World exhibitor announcements","threshold":"ODM per-outlet-managed PDU quotes ≥30% below the entrant's achievable unit cost","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Run a design-in probe with 3 CE rack integrators: submit the product to their qualification process and log the actual selection criteria and thresholds","type":"information","voiNodeId":"cust.oem","note":"Replaces the unsourced 0.22 addressability premise with observed gate criteria"},{"action":"Differentiate on the one axis neither incumbents nor ODMs own regionally: MID-compliant billing-grade metering and local engineering support for CE colo sub-metering requirements","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":"A regulatory-technical feature can reopen brand-spec gates that price and ecosystem cannot"},{"action":"Offer integrators a co-branded (not white-label) program with shared support obligations, splitting the difference between the two closed routes","type":"contractual","voiNodeId":null,"note":null}]},{"id":"intelligent-pdu-concentration-shrinks-serviceable","title":"In the intelligent-PDU sub-market the venture actually plays in, Legrand's dual acquisition plus DCIM spec-lock makes concentration — and the serviceable haircut — worse than the headline","narrative":"shape.cr3 = 0.55 is computed for the whole rack-PDU category, and serviceableFactor's 0.55 haircut is justified purely on regulatory reach (EN 50600, ecodesign, CE/TÜV). Neither number describes the venture's actual product market: INTELLIGENT PDUs, where Legrand owns both specialist brands (Raritan and Server Technology), Vertiv holds Geist, and Schneider leads the category — so the intelligent sub-segment is materially more concentrated than the blended 0.55 suggests. Concentration in intelligent PDUs is not just competitive pressure; it is a serviceability constraint, because intelligent-PDU purchases are spec-locked to DCIM/monitoring ecosystems and vendor-certified compatibility lists. Demand written into specs as 'Raritan-compatible' or 'certified for our DCIM' is not reachable at any price. The 0.55 serviceableFactor, built only on regulatory logic, silently assumes all regulatory-clear demand is spec-open — the one assumption the sub-market's ownership structure contradicts.","category":"model-structure","targetNodes":["serviceableFactor"],"mechanism":[{"step":"trigger","text":"Legrand consolidates the intelligent-PDU specialist brands (Raritan AND Server Technology) while Vertiv holds Geist — the intelligent sub-segment's effective concentration exceeds the blended rack-PDU CR3 of 0.55, which is itself extrapolated from a single 15.8% datapoint","refs":["digest:cr3-vendors#c4","digest:cr3-vendors#c0","ledger:shape.cr3"]},{"step":"propagation","text":"Intelligent-PDU procurement is written against DCIM/monitoring ecosystems: buyer's guides treat DCIM compatibility as a primary selection criterion and the intelligent-PDU sub-market carries only qualitative 'Medium' concentration labels with no share table (Omdia's vendor-share data is paywalled) — so the spec-locked fraction of demand is real but unmeasured","refs":["digest:cust-mix#c13","digest:cr3-vendors#c12","digest:cr3-vendors#c10"]},{"step":"model-impact","text":"serviceableFactor's 0.55, derived solely from the regulatory stack, omits an ecosystem-compatibility haircut; applying it drops effective serviceable share toward 0.40 — the venture's SAM overstates reachable demand even where every certificate is in hand","refs":["ledger:serviceableFactor","profile:Product"]}],"whyMissable":"The team has a serviceable haircut AND a concentration figure, so both boxes look ticked. What hides the risk is scope mismatch: the CR3 describes a broader category than the product, the serviceableFactor describes a narrower constraint (regulation) than reality, and no ledger node connects them — the DCIM spec-lock falls exactly into the seam between two nodes that each look individually handled.","expectedObviousness":"medium","falsifier":"The Omdia rack-PDU vendor-share table showing intelligent-PDU top-3 combined share below ~50%, or ≥3 CE operator/colo PDU specifications requiring only open protocols (SNMP/Redfish/Modbus) with no vendor-certified DCIM compatibility list.","likelihood":{"value":0.35,"rationale":"The ownership consolidation is documented fact and DCIM compatibility is a documented selection criterion, but the actual spec-locked fraction of CE demand is unmeasured — the perturbation size rests on judgment over hard shares, which the paywalled Omdia table could resolve either way.","basis":"evidence"},"perturbation":[{"nodeId":"serviceableFactor","op":"set","value":0.4,"note":"Regulatory-clear but DCIM/spec-locked demand removed from the serviceable share"}],"indicators":[{"signal":"DCIM/compatibility language in CE colo and enterprise PDU specifications","where":"TED tender technical annexes; colo operator technical-standards documents (Equinix/NTT design guides); Data Centre World CE conference specs sessions","threshold":"Majority of sampled specs naming vendor-certified DCIM compatibility rather than open protocols","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"Legrand intelligent-PDU portfolio integration moves (Raritan/Server Technology product-line merges, unified DCIM certification programs)","where":"Legrand investor day materials;
